mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in unemployment rate above 9.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days due to heightened recession risks.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or unemployment declines without corresponding equity gains.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates strong economic demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings prospects.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in co-expansionary periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the correlation between GDPC1 and PAYEMS drops below 0.6.

mechanism_narrative: A monetary tightening cycle marked by FEDFUNDS exceeding 4.5% compresses liquidity, reducing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising rates, or the predictive power of FEDFUNDS declines below 0.05 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A housing starts surge above 550k (HOUST) signals robust demand for residential construction, boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through sectoral spillovers.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 40% of triggered instances, or housing starts fail to correlate with equity gains at p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 93 signals weakening supply chain capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days due to reduced corporate output.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in INDPRO contraction periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the predictive R-squared falls below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5%) combined with declining retail sales (RSAFS < 360k) signals weak consumer demand, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through earnings pressure.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in stagflation scenarios are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction term between CPIAUCSL and RSAFS loses significance at p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Post-shock recovery phases marked by GDP growth above 2% (GDPC1) drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved investor confidence and corporate investment.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of triggered instances, or GDP growth fails to correlate with equity gains at p < 0.05.
